known_bugs: Billing Core phase_010/run_attempt_014 is stale evidence rather than a live run: worker lifecycle shows T005-DEBUG-1 completed at 2026-06-26 18:15:10 +08:00, but state.json remained stamped at 2026-06-26 17:56:30 +08:00 with active_tasks still containing T005-DEBUG-1.
The old run_attempt_014 parent process used pre-V2.77/V2.78 controller logic, so its task-dispatch behavior should not be used to judge the current checkout.
T004 still needs a fresh Billing Core resume boundary under the current checkout to determine whether any remaining failure is unfinished repository work or another controller/runtime defect.
Historical upstream instability existed on 2026-06-25, but the latest 2026-06-26 network smoke passed; there is no current evidence that network availability is the blocking factor.

Billing Core real-worker shells still do not inherit a PATH entry for Go by default even though go.exe is installed at C:\Users\T14S\tools\go-1.26.3\go\bin\go.exe; recovery relaunches must inject that path or equivalent tool discovery until the environment is normalized.

Billing Core T004 remains the real current project blocker: run_attempt_015 stopped on non-partial blockers B-T004-2 and B-T004-3, with T005 and T005-DEBUG-1 still pending and intentionally undispatched.

Before V2.81, the full-roadmap executor treated all phase blockers as non-repairable, which prevented Alchemy from opening a new autonomous repair attempt after T004 technical blockers. V2.81 addresses this for implementation/test blockers while preserving stops for preflight, credentials, recovery, operator, and live-worker blockers.
Before V2.82, the full-roadmap resume scanner could fall back from a newer terminal run_attempt_015 to older stale run_attempt_014 active-task evidence. V2.82 prevents that fallback.

Before V2.83, Windows real Codex workers still treated workspace-write sessions as read-only and rejected patch/verification commands by policy; V2.83 uses the official Codex bypass flag for Windows workspace-write workers, disables plugin sync noise, and relies on Alchemy's isolated worktree plus file-boundary audit controls.

Before V2.84, a real worker timeout created a same-scope debug task, and a debug timeout reset the parent task for replay. Billing Core run_attempt_019 reproduced this as T002 timeout, T002-DEBUG-1 timeout, then T002 replay; V2.84 records a non-partial technical blocker instead.
